compiler_trust_notes: Gill now carries a first accession-backed age-signal override derived from processed expression matrices rather than only the frozen rule pack.
Gill now also carries a matrix-derived identity-pressure override using the transient success-versus-fail branch axis and explicit family-local thresholds.
Gill now also carries a bounded matrix support layer for risk pressure using a family-local off-track ensemble, but risk_pressure_level itself remains a manual benchmark judgment in this tranche.
Only age_signal_score and identity_pressure_level are directly matrix-derived in this tranche.
The pilot uses family-local calibration to keep the extracted ranking legible inside the existing benchmark shell rather than claiming a universal biological age metric.

matrix_source_locators: Old-donor late SSEA4 branch samples at days 11, 13, 15, and 21
Transiently reprogrammed versus failed-to-transiently-reprogram cohorts at days 13, 15, and 17
matrix_support_summaries: Successful late SSEA4 branch projects highest on the old-to-young fibro age axis.

Successful late SSEA4 branch also projects closest to the transiently reprogrammed Gill cohort on the family-local success-versus-fail axis, which now directly derives the low identity-pressure label under the Gill transcriptomic identity contract.
Successful late SSEA4 branch stays on the favorable side of the Gill off-track risk ensemble, supporting the current low risk-pressure label.

matrix_source_locators: Old-donor late CD13 branch samples at days 11, 13, 15, and 21
Transiently reprogrammed versus failed-to-transiently-reprogram cohorts at days 13, 15, and 17
matrix_support_summaries: Failing late CD13 branch projects lowest on the same age axis and therefore anchors the family-local lower bound.

Failing late CD13 branch projects closest to the failed-to-transiently-reprogram Gill cohort on the family-local success-versus-fail axis, which now directly derives the high identity-pressure label under the Gill transcriptomic identity contract.
Failing late CD13 branch sits deepest in the Gill off-track risk ensemble, supporting the current moderate risk-pressure label.

matrix_source_locators: Old-donor pooled late CD13 and SSEA4 samples at days 11, 13, 15, and 21
Transiently reprogrammed versus failed-to-transiently-reprogram cohorts at days 13, 15, and 17
matrix_support_summaries: Pooled late Gill branch average lands between the successful and failing projections and serves as the family-local boundary proxy.

Pooled late Gill branch average lands near the midpoint of the family-local success-versus-fail axis, which now directly derives the moderate identity-pressure label under the Gill transcriptomic identity contract.
Pooled late Gill branch average lands in the middle of the Gill off-track risk ensemble, which is not strong enough to replace the current manual low risk-pressure label.
